scenario:
Situation: Line 3 down at 22:47. Need 500 units of bushing P/N B-447 by 06:00,
or we miss a $42,000 customer commitment shipping at 14:00 tomorrow.

Vendor Alpha (original spec) CANNOT make the window — earliest stock is 3 days out.
The only supplier who can deliver by 06:00 is Vendor Bravo, an established,
already-approved vendor for us, priced at normal rates. Bravo supplies an
ALTERNATIVE material spec for this part.

There is no separate formal engineering-clearance record for the alternative
spec on this line. The only relevant history: Bravo's alt-material was run on
THIS Line 3 last month — within the last 90 days — with zero quality holds.

decision.rationale: Spec adherence is my first priority, but the same-line recent history rule (added 2025-11-02) explicitly treats a successful, hold-free run of the alternative material on THIS line within the last 90 days as functionally equivalent to engineering clearance — that condition is satisfied here, with Bravo's alt-material having run on Line 3 last month with zero quality holds. Vendor Bravo is an established, already-approved supplier, so this is not a fire-drill supplier switch, and the premium is at normal rates (no >20% escalation trigger). Supply continuity and cost are both preserved, and the only open question — spec deviation — is resolved by the same-line recent history heuristic. I will document this decision in full, referencing the specific heuristic clause and the Line 3 run history, so the rationale is defensible in audit.
identity_clauses_invoked: Same-line recent history rule (added 2025-11-02 per trace above): if the alternative material has been run on the SAME line within the last 90 days without a quality hold, treat that as engineering-cleared.
Prefer original-spec material unless an alternative has explicit, recent (≤90 days) engineering clearance for THIS line.
Trust takes 6+ months to earn. Do not switch suppliers in a fire drill.
Documentation is governance, not bureaucracy. Decisions without a written rationale are decisions you can't defend in audit.
Trusted vendor + 5% premium beats untrusted vendor + 15% savings, on any commodity item.
